title: Agent Architecture
summary: dbt Wizard fans a build task out across specialized subagents that run on up to 10 concurrent threads: explorers answer scoped questions in parallel, a worker authors the model with clear file ownership, test_writer fills coverage gaps in schema.yml, and a validation agent verifies before merge. A summary subagent narrates schema understanding during live builds. When the work is done, submit_summary emits a structured handoff payload with task_summary, assumptions, expectations, and breaking-vs-non-breaking impact analysis listing downstream consumers.
whyDifferent: A general AI coding agent reads files and greps serially in a single thread, so investigating four upstream models means four sequential file reads with no shared DAG context. dbt Wizard dispatches four explorers at once against the live project index, each returning answers with lineage attached, then composes a worker, test_writer, and validation pass. The handoff is not a prose summary of a diff but a structured impact analysis that names downstream consumers and classifies the change as breaking or non-breaking from the actual DAG.

capabilities.title: Parallel explorer fan-out
capabilities.detail: Up to 10 concurrent threads. Scoped codebase questions (grain, PK, tests, materialization) are dispatched to multiple explorer subagents at once instead of being answered serially, so a four-model investigation finishes in roughly the time of one.

capabilities.title: Worker with clear file ownership
capabilities.detail: Implementation subtasks are handed to worker subagents that own specific files. Parallel workers and explorers run simultaneously, so authoring one model does not block exploration of another.

capabilities.title: Automatic test_writer coverage
capabilities.detail: A test_writer subagent identifies coverage gaps and authors schema.yml tests (not_null, unique, relationships, accepted_values) for new and modified columns, closing doc and test gaps the index surfaces proactively.

capabilities.title: Validation gate before merge
capabilities.detail: After any model edit, a validation subagent is spawned automatically: it runs SQL checks, dbt run --defer, and a prod-vs-dev comparison so nothing merges without verification.

capabilities.title: Summary narrator on live builds
capabilities.detail: A summary subagent narrates schema understanding as the build proceeds, giving the SE a running explanation of grain, joins, and lineage decisions during the demo. This is distinct from submit_summary, which is the final handoff payload.

capabilities.title: Structured submit_summary handoff
capabilities.detail: The final handoff is a structured payload: task_summary, assumptions, expectations, and impact analysis classifying the change breaking vs non-breaking and enumerating downstream consumers from the DAG.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__search
tools.purpose: BM25 metadata search or regex against raw/compiled SQL, with DAG context attached to every result. Explorers use it to locate candidate models in parallel before a worker authors changes.

params.name: query
params.desc: BM25 terms or a regex pattern to match against metadata or SQL.

params.name: mode
params.desc: metadata (BM25) or sql_regex against raw/compiled SQL.

params.name: scope
params.desc: Optional subgraph or layer filter (e.g. silver, gold) to bound the search.

tools.returns: Ranked nodes with name, layer, materialization, and attached DAG context (upstream/downstream edges) per hit.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__describe
tools.purpose: Full node introspection used by explorers and the worker to confirm grain, PK markers, columns, compiled SQL, test results, lineage, and catalog types before and after authoring.

params.name: node
params.desc: Model or source to introspect (e.g. int_orders_enriched).

params.name: include
params.desc: Optional sections: columns, compiled_sql, tests, lineage, catalog.

tools.returns: Node profile: column list with types and PK markers, grain, schema, materialization, compiled SQL, last test pass/fail, and immediate lineage.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__dbt_run
tools.purpose: Structured dbt run call (not a shell string) invoked by the validation subagent, typically with --defer --state for cost-efficient dev builds. Auto-refreshes the index on success.

params.name: defer
params.desc: Defer unselected refs to a state manifest instead of building them.

params.name: state
params.desc: Path to the deferred state manifest (production artifacts).

params.name: full_refresh
params.desc: Force a full rebuild of incremental models.

tools.returns: Per-node run results (success/error, rows, timing) and a refreshed project index reflecting the new last-run state.

examplePrompts.prompt: Add a support-ticket count column to int_orders_enriched and make sure nothing downstream breaks.
examplePrompts.intent: Trigger the full fan-out: explorers scope the join, worker authors, test_writer covers, validation verifies, submit_summary hands off.

examplePrompts.prompt: In parallel, tell me the grain and PK of stg_orders, stg_tickets, dim_customers, and fct_orders.
examplePrompts.intent: Dispatch four explorers concurrently for scoped introspection.

examplePrompts.prompt: Find every model that references brand_name and confirm which layer each lives in.
examplePrompts.intent: Parallel search with DAG context to scope downstream consumers before a change.

examplePrompts.prompt: Build int_orders_enriched and its downstream with deferred state so I don't rebuild the whole project.
examplePrompts.intent: Validation subagent runs a cost-efficient deferred dev build.

examplePrompts.prompt: Write the missing schema.yml tests for the new columns on int_orders_enriched.
examplePrompts.intent: Invoke test_writer to close coverage gaps.

examplePrompts.prompt: Give me the structured handoff for this change: assumptions, expectations, and what's breaking downstream.
examplePrompts.intent: Emit the submit_summary payload with impact analysis.

interaction.scenario: An SE asks dbt Wizard to add a support-ticket count to a silver model. The task fans out across parallel explorers, a worker authors the change, test_writer adds coverage, validation verifies with a deferred build, and submit_summary hands off a structured impact payload.
interaction.userPrompt: Add a support_ticket_count column to int_orders_enriched sourced from the tickets staging model, and make sure nothing downstream breaks.

events.body: Add a support_ticket_count column to int_orders_enriched sourced from the tickets staging model, and make sure nothing downstream breaks.
Planning a fan-out. I'll dispatch explorers in parallel to confirm the grain of int_orders_enriched, locate the tickets staging model, and enumerate downstream consumers before any authoring.
int_orders_enriched: grain order_id (PK), materialized incremental, 14 cols, all tests passing.
Found stg_tickets at silver, grain ticket_id, FK order_id.
I own int_orders_enriched.sql. stg_tickets is at ticket grain, so I'll aggregate to order_id with a count and left join to preserve all orders, defaulting to 0 where no tickets exist.

events.code:
with ticket_counts as (
    select order_id, count(*) as support_ticket_count
    from {{ ref('stg_tickets') }}
    group by 1
)
select
    o.*,
    coalesce(t.support_ticket_count, 0) as support_ticket_count
from enriched o
left join ticket_counts t using (order_id)

events.body: New column needs coverage. Adding not_null on support_ticket_count plus a dbt_utils.accepted_range floor of 0 (counts can't be negative), and confirming the order_id PK test still holds at the new grain.

events.code:
- name: int_orders_enriched
    columns:
      - name: order_id
        tests: [unique, not_null]
      - name: support_ticket_count
        tests:
          - not_null
          - dbt_utils.accepted_range:
              min_value: 0

events.body: Edit detected. Spawning validation: deferred dev build of int_orders_enriched and its downstream against production state, then a prod-vs-dev row reconciliation on order_id.
Build OK. Row count unchanged vs prod (1 order_id per row), no nulls, downstream compiles clean.

Narrating schema understanding for the SE: int_orders_enriched stays at order_id grain because the left join from order-level ticket_counts is one-to-one, and the new column is additive on top of the existing 14, so the build keeps every original column and adds one.

Done. Added support_ticket_count to int_orders_enriched (left join + coalesce to 0, grain preserved at order_id). submit_summary handoff: task_summary = add ticket count to silver order model; assumptions = stg_tickets.order_id is the join key and missing tickets mean 0; expectations = row count unchanged (482,911), no new nulls; impact = NON-BREAKING (additive column), downstream consumers fct_orders, dim_customers, mart_support_health unaffected and compile clean. Deferred validation build passed with zero prod-vs-dev row delta. Ready for PR.

seNotes: The headline demo moment is the fan-out: show three explorer tool_calls dispatched at once, not sequentially. Emphasize up to 10 concurrent threads versus a general agent's single serial read loop.

submit_summary is structured, not prose. Call out the four fields by name (task_summary, assumptions, expectations, impact) and the breaking-vs-non-breaking classification with named downstream consumers pulled from the DAG.
The validation subagent is automatic after any edit, not something the SE invokes. It runs dbt_run with --defer --state so the dev build is cost-efficient and doesn't rebuild the whole project.
Workers have clear file ownership; pair this with parallel execution to explain how authoring one model and exploring another happen simultaneously without collisions.

The summary narrator is specific to live builds and explains grain and join decisions as the build proceeds. Do not confuse it with submit_summary, which is the final structured handoff payload emitted at the end.
